description: Parser-differential spoof shape: a trusted authserv-id reports DMARC pass, but the From domain it evaluated (header.from=evil.test) differs from the From the recipient parses (example.com) — a DMARC pass badge applied to a domain the user never sees. SPF smtp.mailfrom, DKIM header.d, and Message-ID all align with the visible From, isolating the header.from-vs-From comparison. dmarcHeaderFromMatchesFromDomain is false and a single low-severity dmarc.headerFromMismatch signal is emitted carrying the visible From domain, all trusted+passing header.from domains, and the mismatching subset. Low severity reflects that exact comparison (no PSL/org-domain logic) can also flag benign subdomain/org differences.

authentication-results: mx.example.net; dmarc=pass header.from=evil.test; spf=pass smtp.mailfrom=example.com; dkim=pass header.d=example.com

authenticationResults.raw: mx.example.net; dmarc=pass header.from=evil.test; spf=pass smtp.mailfrom=example.com; dkim=pass header.d=example.com

signals.message: DMARC header.from domain differs from the visible From domain.
